question: Comment detecter les echecs silencieux (hallucinations, package invention, semantic drift) dans le code genere par agent ?

principles.text: Deployer un pipeline de detection specifique aux silent-failure classes : (1) package-hallucination detection via Socket, Snyk dependency audit, pip-audit/npm audit (base rate empirique 19.7% hallucination sur 576k samples, 21.7% OSS / 5.2% commercial) ; (2) semantic-drift detection via regression testing sur behavior non-touche ; (3) SAST/DAST declenches sur agent diffs specifiquement ; (4) runtime observability (GlitchTip, Sentry, OpenTelemetry) pour attraper les effets post-merge. Le CI test suite seul est insuffisant car il ne detecte pas les API parameters hallucines (Knowledge Conflicting Hallucinations) qui sont type-valid.

sources.name: Spracklen et al. 2024 - We Have a Package for You (arXiv:2406.10279)
sources.quote: package hallucinations represent a novel form of package confusion attack that poses a critical threat to the integrity of the software supply chain

sources.name: Socket - The Rise of Slopsquatting blog
sources.quote: If you've ever seen an AI recommend a package and thought, 'Wait, is that real?'—you've already encountered the foundation of the problem

sources.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 §9.1 Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation

etd_balance.benefices: Spracklen 19.7% hallucination rate -> detection evite supply-chain attacks
